nodes.label: AI as well-scoped task handler
nodes.label_negative: AI handling ambiguous or large tasks
nodes.grounding_span: I'll usually hand off a small, well-scoped task to Claude to handle

nodes.label: AI code style alignment
nodes.grounding_span: one of my biggest annoyances for AI-generated code is having it be so off-style that I can't integrate it without significant manual work

nodes.label: Prompting overhead vs manual coding
nodes.grounding_span: It usually comes down to time spent prompting vs. coding

nodes.label: AI as tedious multi-file task handler
nodes.grounding_span: it's faster for me to write the prompt than make a bunch of tedious changes

nodes.label: AI integration pressure
nodes.grounding_span: I do feel the pressure of having to integrate AI as much as possible just to keep up

nodes.label: AI as seamless tool
nodes.label_negative: AI as notable or disruptive tool
nodes.grounding_span: the fact that I don't really think about the AI tools despite using them every day seems like they're doing a good job of just doing their job

nodes.label: Skill development toward AI orchestration
nodes.grounding_span: I've actually spent a lot less time practicing things like writing code by hand and have shifted more to focusing on learning how to use AI more effectively

nodes.label: Iterative refinement through friction points
nodes.grounding_span: When I notice something in an output that annoys me enough to fix it manually, I'll also try to add it to CLAUDE.md to try to avoid the issue in the future
